en: Not knowing a word that is key for the message you're trying to communicate is going to break the conversation.
ko: 전달하려는 메시지에 핵심 단어를 모르면 대화가 끊겨.

en: However, that won't happen if you know how to work your way around it. This is basically what paraphrasing is.
ko: 하지만 돌려 말하는 법을 알면 괜찮아. 이게 바로 패러프레이징이야.

en: For example, you might describe a profession by saying the person who works in a hospital and helps a doctor.
ko: 예를 들어, 병원에서 일하고 의사를 돕는 사람이라고 직업을 설명할 수 있어.

en: The other person then will know you're talking about a nurse. Maybe I'll say the place where people go when they want to buy things.
ko: 그러면 상대방은 간호사를 말하는 거라고 알아. 사람들이 물건을 사러 가는 곳이라고 할 수도 있지.

en: Oh, I know what you mean, a store. No, it's bigger and you can buy all kinds of different things there, especially food.
ko: 아, 가게? 아니, 더 크고 온갖 것들을 살 수 있어, 특히 식품을.

en: Oh, a supermarket. Paraphrase when you can't remember a word to convey more subtle details as well as to organize and manage what you say.
ko: 아, 슈퍼마켓! 단어가 생각 안 날 때 돌려 말해봐. 세부사항도 전달하고 말도 정리할 수 있어.
